data.subject: Floor Update – 3 Votes
data.bodyText:
At approximately 1:30 p.m., the House will reconvene and take the following votes:

  1. Motion on Ordering the Previous Question (H.Res. 1345) – Democrats are urged to vote no on the Previous Question. Voting no will allow House Democrats to offer an amendment to the Rule that would make in order Rep. Raskin's amendment prohibiting anyone who was convicted of or pleaded guilty to assaulting a law enforcement officer during the insurrection at the Capitol on January 6, 2021, from receiving a taxpayer-funded settlement – VOTE NO – 15 minutes
  2. H.Res. 1345 – Rule providing for consideration of S. 2 – Secure America Act; H.R. 8312 – Fraud Prevention and Accountability Act; H.R. 8464 – Stopping Fraudulent Payments Act; and H.Res. 1335 – Condemning actors seeking to defraud the United States Government, and expressing the sense of the House of Representatives that governmentwide fraud and improper payment prevention reforms will meaningfully improve the financial prosperity of the United States, and that Federal program eligibility should be verified before payment – VOTE NO – 5 minutes
  3. Democratic Motion to Discharge H.Res. 1140 – Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 5408) to accelerate workplace time-to-contract under the National Labor Relations Act (Rep. Norcross' Discharge Petition) (Rep. Norcross – Rules) – VOTE YES – 5 minutes

After the first vote series, if the Democratic Motion to Discharge is adopted, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.Res. 1140 – Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 5408) to accelerate workplace time-to-contract under the National Labor Relations Act (Rep. Norcross' Discharge Petition).

Following debate on H.Res. 1140, the House will begin one hour of debate on S. 2 – Secure America Act.

After debate on S. 2, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 7892 – No Aid for Ghost Students Act of 2026.

At approximately 4:30 p.m., the House will take a second series of votes.

Following the second vote series, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 5408 – Faster Labor Contracts Act (Rep. Norcross' Discharge Petition).

At approximately 6:30 p.m., the House will take a vote on passage of H.R. 5408. This will be the last expected vote of the day.

Next votes predicted: at approximately 4:30 p.m., on passage of H.R. 7892, the motion to commit and passage of S. 2, and adoption of H.Res. 1140.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Floor Schedule Update
data.bodyText:
The House has completed debate on H.R. 1329 – Smithsonian American Women’s History Museum Act.

The House has now begun one hour of debate on H.R. 6047 – Sharri Briley and Eric Edmundson Veterans Benefits Expansion Act of 2026.

Following debate on H.R. 6047, the House will recess.

At approximately 4:30 p.m., the House will reconvene and take votes. These will be the last votes of the week.

Next/Last votes predicted: at approximately 4:30 p.m., on the motion to recommit and passage of H.R. 1329; adoption of H.Con.Res. 86 – Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran; the motion to recommit and passage of H.R. 1041 – Veterans 2nd Amendment Protection Act; and passage of H.R. 6047.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Floor Update – 6 Votes
data.bodyText:
At approximately 11:30 a.m., the House will take the following votes:

  1. Adoption of H.Con.Res. 40 – Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran – VOTE YES – 15 minutes
  2. Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 6409 – VOTE YES – 5 minutes
  3. Passage of H.R. 6409 – FENCES Act (Rep. Pfluger – Energy and Commerce) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes 
  4. Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 6398 – VOTE YES – 5 minutes
  5. Passage of H.R. 6398 – RED TAPE Act (Rep. Joyce (PA) – Energy and Commerce) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes
  6. Adoption of H.Res. 965 – Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 1689) to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status (Rep. Pressley's Discharge Petition) – VOTE YES – 5 minutes

After the first vote series, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 1689 – To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status (Rep. Pressley's Discharge Petition).

At approximately 1:30 – 1:45 p.m., the House will take a second series of votes.

Following the second vote series, the House will begin one hour of debate on the Rule providing for consideration of H.R. 8035 – To amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 through October 20, 2027, and for other purposes.

At approximately 3:15 p.m., the House will take a third series of votes on the Rule.

After the third vote series, if the Rule is adopted, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.R. 8035 – To amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 through October 20, 2027, and for other purposes.

At approximately 5:00 p.m., the House will take a vote on passage of H.R. 8035. This will be the last expected vote of the day.

Next votes predicted: at approximately 1:30 – 1:45 p.m., on passage of H.R. 1689 – To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status (Rep. Pressley's Discharge Petition) and adoption of H.Res. 1156 – Expressing support for tax policies that support working families.

data.subject: Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 7008 – Stop Insider Trading Act
data.bodyText:
Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 7008 – Stop Insider Trading Act – offered by Mr. Magaziner of Rhode Island.

If the Democratic Motion to Recommit is adopted, House Democrats will offer an amendment that would strike the underlying text and replace it with the text of the Democratic stock trading ban bill. Text can be found HERE.

Democrats are urged to VOTE YES on the Motion to Recommit.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027
data.bodyText:
Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027 – offered by Mr. Ryan of New York.

If the Democratic Motion to Recommit is adopted, House Democrats will offer an amendment that would prohibit FY27 NDAA funds or FY27 Department of Defense appropriations from being used for military force in Iran, unless authorized by a formal declaration of war or an authorization for the use of military force. Text can be found HERE.

Democrats are urged to VOTE YES on the Motion to Recommit.
data.staffContact: Jake Bayer, Benjamin Harrison, or Ariel Cohen at x5-2020.

data.subject: Floor Update – 9 Votes – Last Votes
data.bodyText:
The House is now taking the following votes on H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027:

  1. Self #28 – Codifies protections and responsibilities for chaplains and subjects such protections to prosecution under the Uniform Code of Military Justice. This amendment is identical to H.R.8769, introduced by Rep. Keith Self in the 119th Congress – 15 minutes
  2. Issa #30 – Authorizes the Secretary to approve any eligible investor and cleared investment professional who meets the program’s statutory and regulatory requirements to assess potential investment opportunities that match with the Department of War's requirements or potential requirements. It also makes clear that the program is not capped, allowing an unlimited number of qualified investors, cleared personnel, and covered companies to participate at the discretion of the Secretary of War – 2 minutes
  3. Harrigan #44 – Prohibits use of automated speed enforcement cameras on military installations – 2 minutes
  4. Grothman #316 – Requires the Secretary of Defense to submit a report detailing options for reducing the number of civilians employed by the Department of Defense by 200,000 – 2 minutes
  5. Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 8800 – VOTE YES – 5 minutes
  6. Final Passage of H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027 (Rep. Rogers (AL) – Armed Services) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes

    Following these votes, the House will take the following votes:
     
  7. Democratic Motion to Recommit on H.R. 7008 – VOTE YES – 5 minutes
  8. Passage of H.R. 7008 – Stop Insider Trading Act (Rep. Steil – House Administration) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes
  9. Adoption of H.Con.Res. 113 – Establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2027 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2028 through 2036 (Rep. Arrington – Budget) – VOTE NO – 5 minutes

These are the last votes for the day

After last votes, the House will begin one hour of debate on H.Con.Res. 89 – Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran.

Any recorded vote requested on H.Con.Res. 89 will be postponed.

**Members are advised that the text of S. 1383 – SAVE America Act will be added to the NDAA upon passage of the NDAA on the Floor.
